reviews.author: Joanne Koong
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that flushing is expensive due to kernel traversing the cgroup update tree for each CPU, and suggested exploring a general solution to move specific stats from rstat to a separate mechanism where updates can be more expensive but reads can be cheap.
reviews.raw_body:
Sounds good. I'll keep an eye out for the work Yosry's doing on this
and look into hooking it up to writeback if/when his general solution
becomes available.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch disables the default strictlimiting behavior for block devices in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) module, addressing performance issues caused by frequent flushing of cgroup update trees across all CPUs on the system.
